mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.05) or negative mean during rate decline periods over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.4 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that immediately suppress consumer spending expectations and drive negative equity returns within one week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE rises above the 0.4pp threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates synchronized domestic demand growth that reliably pushes S&P 500 returns higher over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to produce a positive rank IC greater than 0.05 over any 24-month rolling period.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during periods of low inflation reflects healthy capacity utilization without pricing pressure, generating positive equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward returns turn negative or statistically insignificant when CPIAUCSL growth exceeds 0.3% despite rising INDPRO.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment growth falling below 100,000 signals weakening economic momentum that immediately triggers negative equity returns within five trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in over 60% of months where PAYEMS growth falls below the 100,000 threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising unemployment and falling payroll employment indicates data revision risk and labor market deterioration that suppresses equity valuations over a quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces a positive rank IC exceeding 0.04 over a 36-month rolling window, contradicting the predicted negative direction.

mechanism_narrative: Strong real GDP growth during periods of high unemployment reflects a productivity-driven recovery that supports corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 returns over one month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative or statistically insignificant when GDPC1 growth is positive but UNRATE remains above 7.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained increases in the index of industrial production reflect expanding economic output that consistently correlates with positive equity market performance over a weekly horizon.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show no significant positive correlation (Rank IC < 0.02) with INDPRO growth over any 24-month rolling period.
